query_strategy_description: 25 web-search queries grouped into 9 buckets that map to the entry-strategy blueprint's decision spine. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Avoid named-firm aggregator results; prefer regulator filings, listed-company disclosures, trade associations, government investment promotion bodies, and primary-source compensation surveys. Compared with market_analysis, this strategy spends fewer queries on macro / sizing and more queries on mechanics: licensing path, capex/opex benchmarks, partner identification, FX/political risk.

query_buckets.purpose: One-page market opportunity context - just enough to anchor the addressable opportunity callout in the exec summary. Drives 07_market_opportunity_sizing.
Identify and size the named target customer segment - who an entrant should chase first. Drives 08_target_customer_segment.
expected_sources: national statistics bureau (BPS / GSO / NSO / PSA / DOSM / SingStat)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 55% / estimate 45% (segment definitions often need KIRA triangulation across multiple anchors)

query_buckets.purpose: Identify the top-5 competitors and their structural advantages an entrant must neutralize. Drives 09_competitors_landscape. Compressed vs market_analysis - one page only, no deep profiles.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} top 5 players market share {{year}} revenue ranking
{{country}} {{industry}} new entrant foreign player launch {{year-2}} to {{year}}

query_buckets.purpose: Foreign ownership caps, sector licensing path, timeline-to-license, application cost. Drives 10_regulatory_licensing.
expected_sources: country investment promotion body (BKPM Indonesia / MIC Vietnam / BOI Thailand / MIDA Malaysia / EDB Singapore / BOI Philippines)
law-firm sector guides with named partners
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 80% / estimate 20% (process timelines often quoted as ranges that need triangulation)

query_buckets.purpose: Channel mix options + per-channel economics for entrants. Drives 11_distribution_gtm_channels.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} distribution channels modern trade e-commerce traditional
{{country}} {{industry}} channel margin distributor markup retailer markup
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (channel-margin data is often estimate-heavy)

query_buckets.purpose: Named JV / distributor / agent candidates. Drives 12_partnership_landscape. THIS BUCKET IS THE ONE THAT MOST DIFFERENTIATES THIS BLUEPRINT - prioritize organisation names with country footprint over generic 'partner landscape' content.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} joint venture foreign partner local conglomerate
{{country}} conglomerates with {{industry}} subsidiary or JV partner
expected_sources: country-specific business-group rankings (Indonesia's Top 50 Family Groups / Thai conglomerate league tables / Vietnam corporate league tables)
trade press M&A and JV coverage
country chambers of commerce member directories
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 65% / estimate 25% / primary 10% (KIRA shortlist scoring blends multiple anchors)
query_buckets.note: Use {{adjacent_industry}} to surface conglomerates that span sectors - SEA conglomerates rarely sit in one industry. Examples: Salim Group (ID) spans F&B + cement + telecom; Charoen Pokphand (TH) spans agri + retail + telecom; San Miguel (PH) spans F&B + power + infrastructure.

query_buckets.purpose: Capex benchmarks for the relevant build (factory / retail rollout / fleet / platform / inventory). Drives 13_capex_requirements.
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} factory build cost OR retail store opening capex {{year}}
expected_sources: listed-company expansion announcements (capex disclosed in MD&A)
industrial estate operators' fact sheets (Kawasan Industri JABABEKA / Amata / VSIP / Iskandar / Hemaraj)
central bank lending data on sector capex
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 55% / estimate 45% (capex is the bucket most often KIRA-estimated by benchmarking from comparable disclosed projects)

query_buckets.purpose: Per-headcount cost benchmarks for the country + key functional roles. Drives 14_opex_staffing_ramp.
queries: {{country}} salary benchmark {{industry}} manager engineer fully loaded cost {{year}}
{{country}} office rental cost Jakarta Ho Chi Minh Bangkok Manila Kuala Lumpur Singapore {{year}}
expected_sources: country compensation surveys with disclosed methodology (Mercer / Robert Walters / Michael Page - read for benchmarks but cite the underlying methodology, not the firm name)
country chambers of commerce HR surveys
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 50% / estimate 50% (compensation triangulation almost always needs KIRA synthesis across 2-3 anchors)
query_buckets.note: Voice rule: when citing Mercer/Robert Walters/Michael Page data, attribute to 'compensation surveys published in {{year}}' or 'cross-firm industry compensation benchmarks' - do NOT name the publishing firm in the report. Same anti-positioning principle as Mordor/Frost.

query_buckets.purpose: FX, political, regulatory, operational risks to populate the 5x5 matrix. Drives 16_risk_matrix.
queries: {{country}} {{currency_code}} FX volatility forecast {{year}} central bank political risk
{{country}} {{industry}} foreign company exit failed entry case studies regulatory change
expected_sources: political risk consultancies' country notes (read for facts; do not cite by firm name)
court filings on foreign-investor disputes (BANI Indonesia / SIAC Singapore)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (risk severity scoring is KIRA synthesis)

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets so later buckets can reference earlier findings - in particular bucket 06 (partners) benefits from knowing bucket 03 (competitors) results to avoid recommending a partner that IS a competitor.
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, etc.) - anti-positioning rule
Discard compensation-survey results that cannot be re-attributed to a methodology disclosure - anti-positioning extends to HR consultancies (Mercer / Robert Walters / Michael Page) in published copy
Prefer results with citeable methodology disclosure
If a number appears in 3+ independent sources within +/- 10%, treat as well-anchored secondary
If a number appears in only 1 source, either find corroboration or downgrade to KIRA estimate with anchor commentary

For partner identification: any organisation name making the shortlist must have a verifiable corporate website, country registration, and at least one third-party reference (trade press, M&A coverage, listed-parent disclosure). No anonymous distributors.
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} x {{ratio}} = {{output}}. Example: 'Indonesia disclosed retail capex USD 1.2M per store [secondary] x 25-store year-3 footprint = USD 30M store-rollout capex [estimate]'.
query_execution_rules.scenario_triangulation_template: For the 3-year P&L scenarios on 15_pnl_scenario, each scenario should anchor to: (a) sizing data from bucket 01, (b) channel ramp curves from bucket 05, (c) opex envelope from bucket 08. Bull / base / bear differ on a single named driver (e.g. market share captured, channel mix, time-to-license).
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file matching the shape of references/research_data_R0152.json, with these additional top-level keys: regulator_named, partner_shortlist, capex_benchmarks, opex_benchmarks, fx_assumptions. Each value tagged with source URL + access date + extraction method.

template_variable_resolution.{{currency_code}}: ISO 4217 (IDR / VND / THB / PHP / MYR / SGD)
template_variable_resolution.{{regulator_body}}: Mapped via lookup: ID->BKPM, VN->MPI/MIC, TH->BOI, PH->BOI Philippines, MY->MIDA, SG->EDB
template_variable_resolution.{{country_industrial_zone}}: Country-specific industrial estate operator names: ID->Kawasan Industri JABABEKA/Karawang, VN->VSIP/Amata Vietnam, TH->Hemaraj/Amata, PH->PEZA zones, MY->Iskandar
template_variable_resolution.{{adjacent_industry}}: One- or two-step adjacent industries for partner identification: e.g. for 'roofing' use 'cement OR steel OR retail-home-improvement'; for 'fintech' use 'banking OR telecom OR retail'. Used in bucket 06 to surface multi-sector conglomerates.
template_variable_resolution.{{candidate_segment}}: Filled iteratively as bucket 02 runs - first-pass segment hypotheses (urban middle-class / SMB manufacturers / etc.)
template_variable_resolution.{{leading_player_candidate}}: Filled iteratively as bucket 03 runs - pre-known players or top-result company names
template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
